title: Cheirolepis drabifolia
taxonomy: Cheirolepis drabifolia belongs to the family Pinaceae, which is a group of coniferous plants commonly known as pine trees. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. Cheirolepis drabifolia is a genus of conifers that dates back to the Mesozoic Era, and it is considered one of the oldest known coniferous plants. Its classification as a conifer is supported by its needle-like leaves and cone-shaped reproductive structures. Cheirolepis drabifolia is often studied by paleobotanists to gain insights into the evolution of coniferous plants.
